title: Three Sum
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return all the triplets `[nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]]` such that `i != j`, `i != k`, `j != k`, and `nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0`.

The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

**Key Insight:** Sort the array first. Then for each element, use two pointers from both ends of the remaining subarray to find pairs that sum to the negation of the current element.

**Avoiding Duplicates:** After sorting, skip duplicate values for the outer loop and for both pointers after finding a valid triplet.

**Constraints:**
- `3 <= nums.length <= 3000`
- `-10^5 <= nums[i] <= 10^5`

examples.explanation: The distinct triplets that sum to zero are [-1,-1,2] and [-1,0,1].
The only possible triplet sums to zero.

editorial: Sort the array O(n log n). For each index i, skip duplicates, then use two pointers left=i+1 and right=n-1. If sum==0 record triplet and skip duplicates on both sides. If sum<0 increment left, else decrement right. Overall O(n^2) time, O(1) extra space.

rexHints.level1: Try sorting the array first — it makes duplicate handling and the two-pointer search much easier.
rexHints.level2: Fix one element at index i and use two pointers (left=i+1, right=end) to find pairs summing to -nums[i]. Move pointers inward based on whether the sum is too small or too large.
rexHints.level3: Sort nums. For each i (skip if nums[i]==nums[i-1]), set left=i+1, right=n-1. While left<right: sum=nums[i]+nums[left]+nums[right]. If 0, push triplet, skip duplicate lefts and rights, advance both. If <0, left++. If >0, right--.
